hero_tagline: Finding the perfect holiday park near Lakes Entrance means discovering a place where your family can create lasting memories together, with activities and amenities that suit everyone's needs and interests.
heading: Best Family Holiday & Caravan Parks in Lakes Entrance
hero_intro:
Planning a family trip to Lakes Entrance? We compared the best family holiday and caravan parks in Lakes Entrance so you can quickly find the right park for your kids, budget and travel style.
Lakes Entrance sits where Australia's largest network of coastal lakes meets the ocean, giving families calm lake swimming, boating and a genuine beach all within a short walk of each other.
local_knowledge: NRMA Eastern Beach is the strongest all-round option, with a jumping pillow, heated pool and movie nights, a short walk from the beach. Echo Beach Tourist Park adds animal petting on-site, a nice touch for younger kids, and sits within walking distance of town. The Ninety Mile Beach ocean side offers patrolled surf swimming, while the lakes themselves are calm and better suited to kayaking, fishing and younger swimmers. A boardwalk links the town to the ocean beach across the channel, a pleasant walk with good photo opportunities. Boat cruises on the lakes run regularly and suit all ages.

if_we_were_booking.label: Facilities and position
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose NRMA Eastern Beach Holiday Park for the jumping pillow, heated pool and movie nights, a short walk from the beach.

if_we_were_booking.label: Animal encounters
if_we_were_booking.text: We'd choose Echo Beach Tourist Park for the on-site petting animals alongside its trampoline and playground, walking distance to town.

if_we_were_booking.label: Our tip
if_we_were_booking.text: Swim the calm lakes with younger kids in the morning, then walk the boardwalk across to the patrolled ocean beach in the afternoon for a proper surf swim. Two different water experiences in one easy day.

faq.question: Is Lakes Entrance good for families?
faq.answer: Yes, the combination of calm lake water, a patrolled ocean beach and a walkable town centre makes it a well-rounded family destination.

faq.question: Which Lakes Entrance park is best for kids?
faq.answer: NRMA Eastern Beach Holiday Park, with a jumping pillow, heated pool and movie nights.

faq.question: Is the ocean beach safe for swimming?
faq.answer: The Ninety Mile Beach section is patrolled in season for surf swimming.

faq.question: Are the lakes good for young children?
faq.answer: Yes, the lakes are generally calm and well suited to paddling, kayaking and younger swimmers.

faq.question: Can you do boat cruises on the lakes?
faq.answer: Yes, cruises run regularly and suit families of all ages, a relaxed way to see the lake system.

faq.question: What are the best free family activities?
faq.answer: The boardwalk across to the ocean beach, the town's foreshore, and lake swimming.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Summer school holidays for full beach and lake conditions, though the town is pleasant across most of the year.

faq.question: What should families do when it rains?
faq.answer: The park games rooms and Echo Beach's animal encounters cover a wet morning.

faq.question: How far is Lakes Entrance from Melbourne?
faq.answer: About 3.5 hours drive east, a popular Gippsland coast destination.

faq.question: How far ahead should you book?
faq.answer: NRMA Eastern Beach books out for school holidays given its facilities, so plan ahead.
